links.targetTitle: Powerhouse documents are stored as an append-only operation log, not state snapshots
Powerhouse documents are stored as an append-only operation log, not state snapshots
Connect-on-Swarm data spans four distinct storage layers with different durability

global.title: Reactor native sync stores every operation twice: once in PGlite, once in PostgreSQL
global.content:
The current Powerhouse default sync path creates 2x duplication by design:

```
CONNECT (browser)                    SWITCHBOARD (server)
PGlite (local)                       PostgreSQL (remote)
  - operations                         - operations (SAME data)
  - documents                          - documents
  - relationships                      - relationships

SyncManager                          SyncManager
  outbox -> GqlRequestChannel -----> inbox -> reactor.load()
  inbox  <- GqlRequestChannel <----- outbox
```

**Every operation exists in BOTH databases.** The SyncManager on each side moves `SyncEnvelope` payloads across the `GqlRequestChannel`, ensuring eventual consistency.

The Switchboard is the authoritative remote — a server someone runs. This is the baseline duplication everything else is compared against. It's not a bug or inefficiency; it's the cost of having a multi-device-sync-capable remote copy.

This 2x pattern is the yardstick: any alternative sync path (Swarm, Swarm+Switchboard, fully decentralized) inherits or exceeds it.
global.description: The default Connect-Switchboard path duplicates every op in both databases. GraphQL moves SyncEnvelopes; Switchboard is the authoritative remote.
